You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 48ffc6e2).

## Issue to Resolve

property-naming flags extension properties
The following code gets flagged with 0.49.1:

```kotlin
val Context.adsDelegateFactory @SuppressLint("WrongConstant") get() =
  applicationContext.getSystemService(SYSTEM_SERVICE_AD_DELEGATE_FACTORY) as AdsDelegateFactory
```

> Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed (standard:property-naming)

I would not expect this, if you're adding a property via an extension and leveraging `get()` since the value can indeed change inside the implementation.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

So when I remove `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant")` it does not get flagged as expected. So there's something weird going on.
Indeed, the annotation causes the check to fail. Same happens with:
```
val Context.adsDelegateFactory private get() =
    applicationContext.getSystemService(SYSTEM_SERVICE_AD_DELEGATE_FACTORY) as AdsDelegateFactory
```
Problem will either be resolved in `0.49.2` only when more pressing bugs need to be fixed. Otherwise it will be fixed in the next normal release.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt`

Now I understand the issue better. Looking at the `hasCustomGetter()` function:

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
    findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
        ?.firstChildNode
        ?.text == "get"
```

This checks if the first child node of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` is "get". But when there's an annotation (like `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant")`) or a modifier (like `private`) on the getter, the first child of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` is the annotation or modifier list, not "get".

For example:
- `get() = ...` - first child is "get" ✓
- `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant") get() = ...` - first child is the annotation ✗
- `private get() = ...` - first child is the modifier list ✗

I need to modify the `hasCustomGetter()` function to look for "get" not just as the first child, but anywhere in the children (or at least check if a child with "get" text exists).

 72→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 73→            ?.let {
 74→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 75→            }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 79→        identifier: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        identifier
 83→            .text
 84→            .takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 85→            ?.let {
 86→                emit(
 87→                    identifier.startOffset,
 88→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 89→                    false,
 90→                )
 91→            }
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
 95→        identifier: ASTNode,
 96→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 97→    ) {
 98→        identifier
 99→            .text
100→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
101→            ?.let {
102→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
103→            }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
107→        findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
108→            ?.children()
109→            ?.any { it.elementType == GET_KEYWORD }
110→            ?: false
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
113→
114→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
115→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
116→            ?.children()
117→            .orEmpty()
118→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
123→
124→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
125→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
126→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
127→            containsValKeyword() &&
128→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
131→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
132→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
133→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
134→            ?.let { identifier ->
135→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
136→            }
137→            ?: false
138→
139→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
140→        treeParent
141→            .children()
142→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
143→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
144→            .any { it.text == identifier }
145→
146→    private companion object {
147→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
148→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
149→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
150→    }
151→}
152→

All tests pass. Let me summarize the fix:

## Summary of the Issue
The `property-naming` rule was incorrectly flagging extension properties when their getter had annotations (like `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant")`) or modifiers (like `private`). The root cause was in the `hasCustomGetter()` function.

## Root Cause
The original implementation:
```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
    findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
        ?.firstChildNode
        ?.text == "get"
```

This checked if the first child node of `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` has text "get". But when there's an annotation or modifier on the getter:
- `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant") get() = ...` - the first child is the annotation, not "get"
- `private get() = ...` - the first child is the modifier list, not "get"

## The Fix
Changed the implementation to look for a `GET_KEYWORD` element type anywhere in the children of the `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR`:

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
    findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
        ?.children()
        ?.any { it.elementType == GET_KEYWORD }
        ?: false
```

This correctly identifies a custom getter regardless of any annotations or modifiers that might precede the "get" keyword.

The fix is minimal and targeted - it only changes the `hasCustomGetter()` function and adds the import for `GET_KEYWORD`.

All tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the fix:

## Summary

### Root Cause
The `hasCustomGetter()` function in `PropertyNamingRule.kt` was incorrectly checking for a custom getter by looking at the **first child node** of `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` and checking if its text was "get":

```kotlin
// Before (incorrect)
private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
    findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
        ?.firstChildNode
        ?.text == "get"
```

When a getter has annotations (like `@SuppressLint("WrongConstant")`) or modifiers (like `private`) before the `get` keyword, the first child of `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR` is no longer "get" - it's the annotation or modifier list. This caused the function to return `false` even when there was a custom getter, leading to false positive lint errors.

### The Fix
Changed the implementation to search for the `GET_KEYWORD` element type anywhere in the children of `PROPERTY_ACCESSOR`, rather than checking just the first child:

```kotlin
// After (correct)
private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() =
    findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)
        ?.children()
        ?.any { it.elementType == GET_KEYWORD }
        ?: false
```

This correctly identifies a custom getter regardless of any annotations or modifiers that might precede the "get" keyword.

### Changes Made
1. Added import for `GET_KEYWORD` in `PropertyNamingRule.kt`
2. Modified `hasCustomGetter()` function to check for `GET_KEYWORD` element type in children

### Testing
- All existing tests continue to pass
- Created new test file `PropertyNamingRuleExtensionPropertyTest.kt` with tests for:
  - Extension property with annotation on getter
  - Extension property with private getter
  - Extension property with annotated private getter
  - Simple extension property with getter
  - Top level val property with annotated getter
  - Object val property with private getter
